key_insight: Machine-learning-assisted simulations show that Al content controls chemical ordering in (MoCrTi)100-xAlx refractory HEAs, producing one or two order-disorder transitions and strongly changing stiffness.
engineering_relevance: By tuning composition to favor beneficial short-range order, designers can raise elastic moduli and tailor high-temperature mechanical performance in refractory alloys.
method: Temperature-dependent thermodynamics and short-range order analysis
evidence_signal: The abstract reports heat-capacity and short-range-order signatures of distinct transitions across multiple compositions, alongside computed elastic constants and moduli that track ordering.
watch_item: Predictions depend on the fidelity and transferability of the machine-learning potential, so experimental validation is needed before alloy-design decisions.
paper_title: Study of ordering in (MoCrTi)$_{100-x}$Al$_x$ refractory high-entropy alloys using machine learning interatomic potential
